title: Attaching apparatus
abstract: An attaching apparatus (10) for attaching a support (100) to a base (102) is disclosed. The attaching apparatus comprises a holding arrangement (11 ) for holding the support. In one embodiment, the holding arrangement comprises a locating member (12) for locating the support on the base. The locating member is configured to be received within the support. A fastening arrangement (14, 18) is provided to fasten the holding arrangement to the base. In another embodiment, the holding arrangement includes a receiving member (24) to receive the support. A sealing member (32) may be provided on the receiving member to seal against the support.
claims: Claims Claims 1. An attaching apparatus for attaching a support to a base, the attaching apparatus comprising a holding arrangement for holding the support, the holding arrangement comprising a locating member for locating the support on the base, the locating member being configured to be received within the support, and a fastening arrangement to fasten the holding arrangement to the base. 2. An attaching apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the holding arrangement further includes a receiving member to receive the support, and a sealing member on the receiving member to seal against the support. 3. An attaching apparatus for attaching a support to a base, the attaching apparatus comprising a holding arrangement for holding the support, the holding arrangement comprising a receiving member to receive the support, a sealing member on the receiving member to seal against the support, and a fastening arrangement to fasten the holding arrangement to the base. 4. An attachi ng apparatus according to claim 2 or 3, wherein the receiving member is disposed around the locating member. 5. An attaching apparatus according to claim 2, 3 or 4 wherein the receiving member has an upper rim, and the sealing member is provided on the upper rim. 6. An attaching apparatus according to claim 5, wherein the sealing member is annular, and wherein the sealing member extends radially inwardly from the upper rim to engage the support. 7. An attaching apparatus according to any of claims 2 to 6, wherein the attaching apparatus includes a securing arrangement for securing the support to the holding arrangement. 8. An attaching apparatus according to claim 7, wherein the securing arrangement is part of a bayonet fitting to cooperate with a securing formation on the support. 9. An attaching apparatus according to claim 8, wherein the securing arrangement comprises an elongate cooperating member to cooperate with the support, the cooperating member projecting from the locating member. 10. An attaching apparatus according to claim 9, wherein the cooperating member comprises an elongate member extending inside and across the receiving member. 11. An attaching apparatus according to claim 9 or 10, wherein the cooperating member extends through the locating member and extends therefrom at regions of the locating member opposite each other. 12. An attaching apparatus according to claim 1 1 , wherein the securing arrangement comprises two elongate cooperating members each extending in a direction substantially opposite to the other. 13. An attaching apparatus according to any of claims 9 to 12, wherein the, or each, cooperating member is mounted on the receiving member, the receiving member defining opposed apertures to receive the, or each, cooperating member. 14. An attaching apparatus according to any of claims 9 to 13, wherein the, or each, cooperati ng member is mounted on the locati ng member, the locati ng member defining opposed apertures to receive the, or each, cooperating member. 15. An attaching apparatus according to any of claims 2 to 14, wherein the fastening arrangement comprises a fastening member and a fastening element to cooperate with the fastening member, the fastening member comprising a first connecting formation, and the fastening element comprising a second connecting formation, the first and second connecting formations being configured to interact with each other to connect the fastening member to the fastening element. 16. An attaching apparatus according to claim 15, wherein the first and second connecting formations comprise corresponding first and second threads. 17. An attaching apparatus according to claim 15 or 16, wherein the fastening element is tubular and extends downwardly from the locating member, the fastening member defining a bore to receive the fastening member. 18. An attaching apparatus according to claim 16 or 17, wherein the fastening element is provided in the receiving member. 19. An attaching apparatus according to claim 18, wherein the fastening element comprises a threaded nut to receive the fastening member. 20. An attaching apparatus according to any of claims 15 to 19, wherein the fastening member is insertable through the base to cooperate with the fastening element, thereby clamping the base between the fastening member and the fastening element. 21. An attaching apparatus according to any of claims 15 to 20, wherein the receiving member has a bottom end portion which defines an aperture through which the locating member can extend, the fastening element extending from the receiving member when the locating member is received through the aperture in the bottom end portion of the receiving member. 22. An attaching apparatus according to any of claims 2 to 21 , further including an indicator arrangement to indicate a desired orientation of the attaching apparatus, the indicator arrangement comprising a pair of projecting formations extending outwardly from the receiving member. 23. An attaching apparatus according to claim 22, wherein the projecting formations are attached directly to the receiving member, or are provided on a mounting member to mount the projecting formations on the receiving member. 24. An attaching apparatus according to any preceding claim, comprising a carrying arrangement for carrying an elongate article, the carrying arrangement comprising an outwardly extending elongate carrying member. 25. An attaching apparatus according to claim 24, wherein the carrying arrangement comprises mounting means to mount the outwardly extending carrying member on the holding arrangement, the connecting means being configured to mount the outwardly extending member on the receiving member. 26. An attaching apparatus according to claim 25, wherein the mounting means comprises a collar extending around the receiving member. 27. An attaching apparatus according to claim 25 or 26, wherein the mounting means further includes a holder to hold the carrying member, the holder defining a through hole, through which the carryi ng member extends. 28. An attaching apparatus according to any of claims 24 to 27, wherein the carrying arrangement comprises an affixing member for affixing the carrying member to the elongate article, the affixing member comprising a body defining a through hole, through which the carrying member can extend. 29. An attaching apparatus according to claim 28, wherein the affixing member has flange portions extending from the body by means of which the affixing member can be attached to the article. 30. An attaching apparatus according to any of claims 24 to 29, wherein the carrying arrangement comprises a strap formation arrangeable between the holding arrangement and the article, the strap formation comprising a first securing member mountable on the holding arrangement, and an elongate second securing member which can extend from the first securing member to the article. 31. An attaching apparatus according to claim 30, wherein the first securing member is annular and is configured to extend over the receiving member. 32. An attaching apparatus according to claim 30 or 31 , wherein the second securing member defines a plurality of apertures, through a selected one of which a fastener can extend to fasten the second securing member to the article. 33. A method of attaching a support to a base, wherein the method comprises providing an attaching apparatus as claimed in claim 1 or 3, fastening the holding arrangement to the base by means of the fastening arrangement, and holding the support by the holding arrangement. 34. A method according to claim 33, wherein the holdi ng arrangement comprises a locating member and the step of holding the support comprise locating the support on the locating member by arranging the support so that the locating member is received within the support. 35. A method according to claim 33 or 34, wherein the fastening arrangement comprises a fastening member and the step of fastening the locating member to the base comprises forming a hole in the base, inserting either or both of the fastening member and an end region of the locating member through the hole to attach the fastening member to the locating member. 36. A method according to claim 36, wherein the fastening member and the end region of the locating member are correspondingly threaded, so that the fastening member can be threadably received on the locating member. 37. A method according to any of claims 33 to 36, further including a securing arrangement for securing the support to the locating member, the securing arrangement comprising a bayonet fitting comprising a cooperating member on one of the support and the locating member, and a slot defined in the other of the support and the locating member for receiving the cooperating member. 38. A method according to claim 37, wherein two opposite slots are defined by the locating member or the support. 39. A method according to claim 36. 37 or 38, wherein the step of securing the support to the locating member comprises disposing the support on the locating member so that the locating member is received therein, and turning the support about its longitudinal axis to allow the cooperating members to cooperate with the slots. 40. A method according to any of claims 33 to 39, wherein the attaching apparatus comprises a receiving member for receiving the support, the receiving member extending around the support, and the step of locating the support on the locating member comprises disposing the support between the locating member and the receiving member. 41. A method of installing an elongate article on a building, said method comprising mounting a plurality of attaching apparatus as claimed i n claim 1 or 2 on a base member, wherein each of said attaching apparatus comprises a carrying arrangement, mounting the elongate article on the carrying arrangement of each attaching apparatus, and thereafter lifting the base member and the elongate article fixed to the attaching apparatus on to a desired position on the building. 42. A method according to claim 41 , wherein the base member has an elongate flange member having opposite edge regions, and method includes arrangi ng the attaching apparatus on the flange member. 43. A method according to claim 41 or 42, comprising arrangi ng the elongate article in engagement with the base member. 44. A method according to claim 41 , 42 or 43, wherein the carrying arrangement comprises a strap formation arrangeable between the holding arrangement and the article, the strap formation comprising a first securing member mountable on the holding arrangement, and an elongate second securing member which can extend from the first securing member to the article, and the method comprises securing the first securing member to the holding arrangement by arranging the first securing member so that the holding arrangement is received therethrough. 45. A method according to claim 44, comprising securing the second securing member to the article by selecting one of the plurality of apertures and inserting a fastener through said selected aperture and through the article to secure the second securi ng portion to the article. 46. An attaching apparatus substantially as herein described with reference to Figures 1 to 7 of the accompanying drawings. 47. An attaching apparatus substantially as herein described with reference to Figures 8 and 9 of the accompanying drawings. 48. A method of attaching a support to a base substantially as herein described with reference to Figures 1 to 7 of the accompanying drawings. 49. A method of attaching a support to a base substantially as herein described with reference to Figures 8 and 9 of the accompanying drawings. 50. A method of installing an elongate article on a building substantially as herein described with reference to Figures 10 to 14 of the accompanying drawings.
